2606.06475.title: RREDCoT: Segment-Level Reward Redistribution for Reasoning Models
2606.06475.paper_summary: Recent advancements in reasoning language models have been driven by Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning. Most often, these rely on the Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) algorithm or modifications thereof to steer the models to produce Chain-of-Thought (CoT) traces. The final answer can only be verified, and the reward assigned, after the CoT trace is complete, making it a delayed reward problem. GRPO and its modifications correspond to Monte Carlo methods in standard RL, which are known to suffer from high variance. A possible solution to this problem is the redistribution of rewards through credit assignment, where segments of the CoT trace that are important for arriving at the desirable solution are emphasized by assigning a higher reward. While Monte Carlo sampling can be used to provide an unbiased estimate of intermediate state values, its computational overhead makes it unsuitable for train-time credit assignment in long contexts at high granularity. We introduce RREDCoT (Reward REDistribution for Chain of Thoughts), which utilizes the model itself to approximate the optimal reward redistribution without additional generation. We investigate the advantages of our method compared to MC sampling and several attribution methods. We further analyze several aspects relevant to the construction of the redistribution such as segmentation of CoT traces and state value estimation.
2606.06475.paper_summary_zh: 最近在推理語言模型方面的進展是由強化學習（RL）微調驅動的。這些模型通常依賴於群體相對策略優化（GRPO）算法或其修改版，以引導模型產生思路鏈（CoT）痕跡。最終答案只能在CoT痕跡完成後進行驗證，並分配獎勵，這使得它成為一個延遲獎勵問題。GRPO及其修改版對應於標準RL中的蒙地卡羅方法，這些方法已知存在高方差的問題。解決此問題的一種可能方案是通過信貸分配重新分配獎勵，強調那些對達到理想解決方案至關重要的CoT痕跡片段，並分配更高的獎勵。雖然可以使用蒙地卡羅抽樣來提供中間狀態值的無偏估計，但其計算開銷使其不適合在長上下文中以高粒度進行訓練時的信貸分配。我們引入了RREDCoT（思路鏈的獎勵重新分配），它利用模型本身來近似最佳的獎勵重新分配，而無需額外生成。我們研究了我們的方法與蒙地卡羅抽樣和幾種歸因方法的優勢。我們還分析了與重新分配構建相關的幾個方面，例如CoT痕跡的分段和狀態值估計。
2606.06475.author: Mykyta Ielanskyi et.al.
2606.06475.authors: Mykyta Ielanskyi, Kajetan Schweighofer, Lukas Aichberger, Sepp Hochreiter
